spec_match.rationale: Listens on `beforeinput` and `input` on a contenteditable, inspects `inputType` and `dataTransfer`, and calls `preventDefault()` exactly as the spec describes. The simulated insertions exercise the same code path Android IME media triggers.

interactive.rationale: Live event log, MIME-toggle switches, preventDefault checkbox, single-file and sticker-pack simulated insertions, and a non-editable target probe make this a working monitor.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a live event log with MIME-acceptance toggles and a preventDefault override — all delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers multi-file DataTransfer packs, text/plain fallback via dataTransfer.getData(), rejected MIME paths, preventDefault blocking, and a non-editable target event with absent dataTransfer.

references.rationale: The see-also section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5484282563919872.

design_system.rationale: Log markers are inline SVG with currentColor, generated canvas colors use CSS variables, and local background overrides keep matching text colors.

summary: The monitor now includes the required ChromeStatus link, SVG log markers, multi-file sticker-pack simulation, text fallback readout, and a non-editable target probe that shows why contenteditable focus matters.
